title: ForgeBrain Reel Storyboard
description: The structured, render-ready scene plan produced by the Storyboard Generator from a finalized script. Produced after the Script Generator (see script-spec.md) and consumed directly by the Renderer. See storyboard-spec.md for the full process this schema supports.

topic_id.description: Matches the corresponding id in curriculum/java-roadmap.json and the topic_id of the script this storyboard was built from.

topic_title.description: Matches the corresponding title in curriculum/java-roadmap.json.

total_duration_seconds.description: Sum of every scene's duration. Must equal the last scene's end_time and should land at or below target_duration_seconds.

scene_count.description: Must equal the length of the scenes array.

scenes.description: The ordered scene-by-scene plan. Scenes are contiguous: each scene's start_time equals the previous scene's end_time, with no gaps or overlaps.

scene_order.description: The scene_id values from scenes, in playback order. Must exactly match scenes[].scene_id in the same order — a convenience list for renderers that don't need the full scene objects to sequence playback.

visual_style.description: The overall primary visual technique for this reel. Carried from content_director_output.visual_style unless the storyboard stage has a specific reason to refine it.

animation_style.description: The overall motion energy and approach across scenes, distinct from visual_style (which is about what's shown, not how it moves).

subtitle_style.description: How subtitles are rendered across the reel.

code_style.description: The code *rendering technique* used in code-reveal scenes. Distinct from content_director_output.code_style, which is about narrative framing (e.g. bug-example, optimization-example) — see storyboard-spec.md Section 3 for how the two relate.

transition_style.description: The default transition used between scenes unless a specific scene overrides it via transition_in/transition_out.

emphasis_points.description: The reel's most important visual/narrative moments across the whole timeline — the 'don't miss this' beats, distinct from per-scene highlighted_words.

platform.description: Carried from the script/content strategy unless explicitly overridden.

aspect_ratio.description: Target frame aspect ratio. 9:16 is the default for vertical short-form reels.

render_style.description: The overall brand rendering aesthetic applied across every scene.

target_duration_seconds.description: Carried from the script's target_duration_seconds unless explicitly overridden.

storyboard_version.description: Version of the storyboard process/schema that produced this plan, for future caching and migration.

based_on_script_version.description: The script_version of the script this storyboard was built from, for traceability.

start_time.description: Seconds from the start of the reel.

duration.description: This scene's length in seconds, derived from its voiceover_text word count at the same speaking rate used in script-spec.md Section 8.

scene_type.description: What role this scene plays. Should be chosen from the actual content of this beat, not mechanically assigned from which script field it came from — see storyboard-spec.md Section 6.

voiceover_text.description: The exact spoken text for this scene. Concatenating every scene's voiceover_text in scene_order must reproduce the script's full_spoken_script exactly — no words added, dropped, or reordered.

on_screen_text.description: Short on-screen text overlays for this scene (keywords/captions, not full sentences). May be empty for a scene with no overlay text.

visual_description.description: Prose description of what the viewer sees in this scene, specific enough for a renderer to execute without guessing.

code_block.description: The code shown in this scene, or null if no code is visible.

motion_notes.description: How elements move within this scene (e.g. 'text kinetic-types in from the left, settles center, holds').

transition_in.description: How this scene begins relative to the previous one. Defaults to the storyboard's transition_style unless this scene needs something different.

transition_out.description: How this scene ends relative to the next one.

subtitle_segments.description: The subset of the script's subtitle_segments that fall within this scene, now carrying absolute start_time/end_time on the reel timeline instead of the script's relative estimated_duration_seconds.

highlighted_words.description: Union of emphasis_words across this scene's subtitle_segments, for quick renderer reference without iterating the nested array.

purpose.description: One sentence on the retention/storytelling job this scene does, tying back to the content strategy's retention_goal or emotional_goal.

code_snippet.description: The on-screen code, carried from the script's code_narration.code_snippet.

focus_line.description: Which line to highlight, carried from the script's code_narration.focus_line.

shortest_scene_duration_seconds.description: The shortest scene's duration, for a quick check against the 'avoid long static scenes' guidance.

scene_id.description: Which scene this emphasis point falls within.

moment.description: What specifically happens at this moment (e.g. 'the exception message appears').

reason.description: Why this moment matters for retention, tying back to the content strategy's retention_goal.

overall_confidence.description: Coarse self-assessment of how render-ready this storyboard is.

flagged_uncertainties.description: Scene-timing or visual judgment calls flagged for reviewer attention.

unresolved_conflicts.description: Reserved for future multi-template or platform-format conflicts. Empty until relevant.
